extraction.one_line_summary: HAMILTON Lucas attacked late on the final climb (stage 4 summit finish), soloing to victory over MASNADA who chased but couldn't close.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon attacked immediately at descent base to test GC rivals, revealing Mitchelton's aggressive posture despite no GC ambitions.
VLASOV Alexander countered hard when others hesitated, forcing MICKA/KNOX to bridge rather than let Astana escape unchallenged.
MASNADA Fausto rode solo all day in breaks but couldn't match HAMILTON's late acceleration, suggesting altitude training differential matters.
WOODS Michael took bonus seconds for third despite no stage win attempt, prioritizing GC positioning over collaboration.

rider_signals.HAMILTON Lucas: positive: claims altitude training at Andorra home gave form advantage, first World Tour win suggests emerging climbing capability
rider_signals.MASNADA Fausto: neutral: strong all-day effort but beaten in final sprint, stage win form from 2019 Giro not replicated
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: explicitly noted as 'found wanting' when YATES attacked, concerning signal for former grand tour winner
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexander: positive: aggressive counter-attacking in best young rider jersey, Astana allowing opportunistic moves
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: positive: took bonus seconds tactically for 9-second GC lead, prioritizing overall over stage hunting
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: allowed HAMILTON freedom for opportunistic wins while preserving GC role, Mitchelton dual-threat strategy working
